content: I can't believe I actually left my buying job in San Francisco to work remotely from Pittsburgh. The cost of living difference is insane - I'm saving so much money now.
That's a huge change! What made you decide to leave the Bay Area after all this time?

Honestly, it was a combination of things. The housing costs were killing me - I was paying $3200 for a one-bedroom. Plus I got into drone photography and wanted more space to actually use my equipment.
Drone photography sounds fascinating! How long have you been doing that, and what do you like to photograph?

About 2 years now. I love shooting industrial sites and cityscapes. Pittsburgh has been amazing for that - so many bridges and interesting architecture. I also picked up DJing as a hobby - it's a nice creative outlet that's different from my day job.
That's quite a creative combination! How has the transition been with your work as a fashion buyer? Do you find it harder to stay connected to trends from Pittsburgh?
Surprisingly, it's been fine. Most of my work is online anyway. I'm 36 now and I realized I was burning out in SF. My friend Jordan, who's also in fashion, moved to Austin last year and loves it.
It sounds like you're really thriving with this change. Have you thought about whether you want to stay in Pittsburgh long-term or if this is more of a trial run?

I'm honestly not sure. I love the lifestyle here but I miss some things about SF - like the diversity of restaurants. There's a great Vietnamese place near my old apartment that I think about all the time.
Food can be such a big part of feeling at home somewhere. Have you found any good spots in Pittsburgh that help fill that gap?
There's a place in the Strip District that's decent, but it's not the same. I've been trying to get into the local tennis scene too - there's a group that plays at Frick Park on weekends.
That's great that you're getting involved in the community! How's your speedcubing going? I remember you mentioning that was another hobby of yours.
Oh yeah, I almost forgot about that! I've been doing it less since the move, but I'm trying to get back into it. I actually taught my neighbor's kid how to solve the 3x3 - he's obsessed now.
That's wonderful that you're sharing your hobby! It sounds like Pittsburgh is offering you a lot of new opportunities while still letting you maintain connections to your old interests.
Exactly. I'm still figuring it all out, but I don't regret the move. My rent here is $1400 for a two-bedroom with a home office - I can't even describe how amazing that feels after SF.
That's a huge difference! It sounds like the financial breathing room has given you space to explore these new hobbies and really settle into the community.
Definitely. I've even started getting into origami - it's so meditative after staring at screens all day. My friend from work and I are thinking about starting a little club.
An origami club sounds delightful! It seems like you're really building a new life there while still staying connected to your professional world. That's quite an accomplishment.
